tax: Ember Flameling is placed at species rank beneath Flameling (Pyric order, Fire Elementals class). Diagnosis rests on a fuel-dependent open-flame body, ignition on contact, and instant death without fuel or air. It is separated from its sibling the Smoulder Cinderling by its bright leaping open flame versus the Cinderling's creeping smokeless smoulder; the two share the genus body-plan but never the visible flame. Composition, not colour or menace, fixes the placement: open combustion sustained by ambient fuel gas and oxygen.
ap: Ember Flameling stands 0.4–0.8 m, a leaping flame-ball. Its silhouette is a roughly spherical flame with trailing licks and a brighter core. Three diagnostic surface markers separate it in the field: a colour that shifts with its fuel — orange on oil, blue on gas-seep; heat-shimmer that distorts the passage behind it; and a sharp crackle and the smell of whatever it is burning. It moves with a darting, bobbing flit along fuel gradients, drawn to any new fuel source, and shows bright self-light, the strongest of any elemental. Even after dispersal it can be identified from scorch-rings, soot films, and charred patches along its path, so a survey team need not see the living body to log the species.
eco: Ember Flameling persists in gas-seep vents, lamp-oil stores, fungal methane pockets, and anywhere combustible vapour collects on the worked tiers. It does not breed; it forms where a fire-pressure spark ignites a fuel-rich pocket into a self-sustaining flame-body, and recurs whenever the same vent refills with fuel and the next spark re-ignites it. It sustains its mass on combustion of ambient fuel, drawing in any flammable gas, oil, or fibre it can reach. Range is bounded by the fuelled, oxygenated zone: outside that band the body starves and goes out the instant fuel or air fails. Juvenile or nascent forms appear as a vent-flame that wanders off its source against the draught, which lets a surveyor predict where an adult will rise.
beh: Behaviour is the behaviour of its medium given intent. Ember Flameling is roused by fresh fuel, a draught, or a torch brought near and otherwise lies it cannot rest; without fuel it simply dies. It claims the fuelled pocket it feeds in and answers intrusion with leaping to ignite clothing, oil, and fungal mat. Toward grey-folk it is drawn to lamps and oil; survey crews report that Lanternkin shutter their flames in any gallery that smells of gas-seep. It is not cultural and keeps no craft — its 'decisions' are thresholds of pressure, heat, current, or charge, not plans.
traitsText: Stable characters of Ember Flameling: a body of open combustion sustained by ambient fuel gas and oxygen; immune to fire and heat, and unharmed by piercing or cutting; and a defining weakness — smothering, water, and fuel-starvation, any of which kill it at once. Its senses run through the medium itself: fuel-gradient and heat sensing through the air. It tolerates darkness only in the sense that it makes its own light but fails at loss of fuel, air, or a dousing. Mass varies with available element, yet the proportions, the spherical flame and fuel-colour shift, stay constant, which is why mass alone never reclassifies the taxon.
abilities: Abilities of Ember Flameling are bounded by its anatomy, not borrowed from other elements: it ignites flammable material on contact; it leaps along fuel gradients to spread; it flares brighter and hotter when fed fresh fuel; and it sheds blinding light to dazzle. Each is a direct expression of self-sustaining combustion of ambient fuel, so it cannot, for instance, persist without fuel or air, cross water, or carry mass. The powers scale with body mass and fade as the body disperses.
bg: In the Throat, Ember Flameling matters because it turns every gas-seep and oil store into a spreading-fire hazard, and crews now shutter lamps near fuel-smell. Grey-folk survey practice treats it as a fire hazard logged with fuel pockets and oil stores, and its presence reshapes routes through the gas-seep galleries, now ventilated and lamp-shuttered before entry. A vent-flame that wanders upwind off its source is the Lanternkin's signal to douse all open light.
g: Ember Flameling (Ignis vagus) is a small open-flame elemental that flits as a self-feeding ball of fire, igniting whatever it touches. Documented from the gas-seep vents and lamp-oil stores of the worked tiers, it expresses a single inner element as its whole anatomy — a flickering core of orange flame wrapped in heat-shimmer — rather than as flesh laid over an animal skeleton. It has no solid part at all; its 'body' is the combustion itself, sustained as long as it finds fuel. Field teams record it as a true elemental, not a beast wearing mineral armour: when it disperses it leaves a scorch-ring and a puff of soot where the flame guttered out and no carcass.
